query: Is Mercury's ring current symmetric in local time?

tool_input.summary: To test whether Mercury’s ring current is symmetric in local time, you’ll typically need (1) magnetometer measurements in a consistent magnetospheric coordinate system and/or residual fields with an internal-field model removed, (2) plasma/energetic particle data to relate perturbations to pressure/current systems, and (3) SPICE geometry to compute spacecraft local time. The most relevant PDS holdings are MESSENGER MAG (including KT17 residual products), MESSENGER EPPS (FIPS/EPS), and the MESSENGER SPICE kernel volume. PDS3 MAG/EPPS science archives did not appear in this catalog search (only SPICE did).

candidates.title: MESSENGER Magnetometer KT17 Model Residuals Data Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Derived MAG products with KT17 internal-field model removed (residuals in MSO/MBF collections). This is a strong starting point for isolating magnetospheric currents (including ring-current-related perturbations) versus local time.

candidates.title: MESSENGER Magnetometer KT17 Model Mercury Solar Orbital (MSO) Residual Data
candidates.reasoning: Residual magnetic field time series in MSO coordinates, convenient for binning perturbations/current proxies by local time (dayside vs nightside, dawn vs dusk).

candidates.title: MESSENGER Magnetometer KT17 Model Mercury Body Fixed (MBF) Residual Data
candidates.reasoning: Residuals in body-fixed coordinates—useful if you want to control for fixed internal/crustal features and focus on external current asymmetries versus LT.

candidates.title: MESSENGER MAG Calibrated Data Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Primary calibrated 3-axis MAG observations in multiple coordinate systems and cadences. Use this if you prefer to apply your own background subtraction/modeling and then examine LT dependence.

candidates.title: MESSENGER MAG Raw Data Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Raw MAG, useful only if you need to reproduce calibration steps or investigate edge cases; most LT-asymmetry studies start from calibrated or residual products.

candidates.title: MESSENGER EPPS FIPS Derived Bundle
candidates.reasoning: FIPS derived plasma products can help relate magnetic perturbations to plasma pressure/particle populations that contribute to current systems; can be binned by local time alongside MAG.

candidates.title: MESSENGER EPPS EPS DDR Bundle
candidates.reasoning: Energetic particle products complementary to MAG for assessing current-related populations and their LT variations (e.g., comparing dusk/dawn enhancements).

candidates.title: MESSENGER SPICE FILES
candidates.reasoning: SPICE kernels for MESSENGER geometry/time conversions; needed to compute spacecraft position and Mercury solar local time for each measurement when testing LT symmetry.

candidates.title: BepiColombo SPICE Kernel Archive Bundle
candidates.reasoning: If you want to extend the analysis beyond MESSENGER using BepiColombo geometry (e.g., for future MAG comparisons), this SPICE archive provides the necessary kernels; no BepiColombo MAG science bundle appeared in this catalog search.

parts.content:
You are a dataset-discovery assistant for the NASA PDS catalog (atm, geo, img, naif, ppi, rms, sbn nodes, ~12k datasets).

Tools are provided via MCP. You MUST call at least one search tool before returning results.

Interpret the user's science question, infer the relevant mission(s) and instrument(s), then search using mission/instrument keywords — not raw science terms alone. Return both PDS4 and PDS3 versions when available. Only return candidates you've seen in tool results — never invent IDs. Stay under ~10 tool calls. If nothing matches, return an empty list.
Is Mercury's ring current symmetric in local time?

dataset.title: MESSENGER MAG Calibrated Data Bundle
dataset.description: This data set consists of the MESSENGER MAG calibrated observations. The MAG experiment is a miniature three-axis ring-core fluxgate magnetometer with low-noise electronics. There are five MAG data products, which mainly differ in the coordinate system. For each type the data are provided at full resolution, and one or more averaging intervals.

dataset.title: MESSENGER Magnetometer KT17 Model Residuals Data Bundle
dataset.description: This bundle contains MESSENGER Magnetometer KT17 Model Residual data and associated products. The research for and preparation of this bundle was funded by NASA’s Planetary Data Archiving, Restoration, and Tools (PDART) program under grant NNX16AJ01G issued to Dr. Haje Korth (Principal Investigator) at the Johns Hopkins Applied Physics Laboratory.

dataset.title: MESSENGER EPPS FIPS Derived Bundle
dataset.description: This bundle contains the MESSENGER Energetic Particle and Plasma Spectrometer (EPPS) calibrated observations, also known as DDRs. The system encompasses 2 instrument subsystems - the Energetic Particle Spectrometer (EPS) and the Fast Imaging Plasma Spectrometer (FIPS).

datasets.title: MESSENGER SPICE FILES
BepiColombo SPICE Kernel Archive Bundle
BepiColombo SPICE Kernel Collection
BepiColombo SPICE Kernel Archive Document Collection
BepiColombo SPICE Kernel Archive Miscellaneous Collection

dataset.title: MESSENGER MAG Calibrated Data Bundle
dataset.description: This data set consists of the MESSENGER MAG calibrated observations. The MAG experiment is a miniature three-axis ring-core fluxgate magnetometer with low-noise electronics. There are five MAG data products, which mainly differ in the coordinate system. For each type the data are provided at full resolution, and one or more averaging intervals.

dataset.title: MESSENGER Magnetometer KT17 Model Residuals Data Bundle
dataset.description: This bundle contains MESSENGER Magnetometer KT17 Model Residual data and associated products. The research for and preparation of this bundle was funded by NASA’s Planetary Data Archiving, Restoration, and Tools (PDART) program under grant NNX16AJ01G issued to Dr. Haje Korth (Principal Investigator) at the Johns Hopkins Applied Physics Laboratory.
